title: Citrusy Chewy Thai Shrimp Paste Salad
description: A light, refreshing salad that balances the umami of shrimp paste with zesty lime, a hint of heat, and chewy tapioca pearls, perfect for a quick sick-day meal.

ingredients.name: red chili
ingredients.notes: thinly sliced, seeds removed for less heat

instructions: Boil the shrimp in lightly salted water for 2 minutes until just pink; drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process.
In a small saucepan, heat 1 teaspoon of neutral oil over medium heat. Add the shrimp paste and sliced chili; stir until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Pour in lime juice, fish sauce, and palm sugar. Stir until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is well combined.
Meanwhile, cook the sweet potato starch noodles according to package instructions (usually 5 minutes). Drain and rinse under cold water to set the texture and chill the pearls.
Add the cooled noodles to the shrimp paste mixture. Toss well to coat.
Pour the noodle and shrimp paste mixture into a large bowl. Add the sliced cucumber, chopped cilantro, and the boiled shrimp. Toss gently to combine, ensuring all ingredients are evenly coated.
Serve immediately, garnished with toasted peanuts and an extra squeeze of lime if desired.
